page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is categorized in the bottom 10%, reflecting a lower clock speed tradeoff. However, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a similar divergence: small-block cached reads perform at an average level of 802.27 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached reads and writes are weaker, placing in the bottom 25% at 30.52 GB/sec and 15.5 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource density position this instance as a stable option for workloads requiring predictable multi-threaded execution without the premium of high-frequency compute or specialized hardware. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, Redis database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks where single-core speed is not the primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, clocked at 2.6 GHz, and paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, and large-block memory bandwidth is weak. However, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark scores, and Redis SET operations maintain average middle-50% performance. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core speeds. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, offering balanced multi-core performance with lower single-core speeds.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 64 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for predictable multi-threaded workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Lower single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded workloads
